governing_requirement: Authenticate and replay-protect Main consumer requirements, normalize supported versions, labels, person/media/transform scope, authority floors, resource envelopes and required evidence, and distinguish required from optional unmet capabilities without accepting ambiguity or unknown semantics.
prerequisites: MF-P6-09.07 remains open; the producer admission decision must resolve the adopted crosswalk and exact accepted producer schema identities.
existing_enforcement: The frozen requirements schema is strict, canonical-hashed and Ed25519-signed by the consumer_requirements role with nonce and bounded authentication timestamps.

It requires the exact 12 schema names, both trust authorities, unique capability IDs, required versus optional capability arrays, labels/media/person/transform/authority/resource envelopes, and fail-closed runtime flags.
Validation rejects duplicate trust authorities/key IDs, invalid key time ordering, non-exact operational certificate kinds, p50 greater than p95, and request budgets shorter than queue plus p95.
Adoption validation later compares declared requirements against the supplied release and capability snapshot.

confirmed_gaps.observed: validate_maskfactory_consumer_requirements has no at_time or replay-state input and validates authentication at created_at. The fixture expires at 00:05Z but the same document remains validator-clean at later producer use unless another layer supplies a ledger.

confirmed_gaps.probe: Substitute the embedded MaskFactory key_set_sha256 and trusted re-sign the requirements.
confirmed_gaps.interpretation: The signer is authenticated, but the embedded trust registry/policy identities are not self-hashed or compared to the producer-adopted registry.

confirmed_gaps.probe: Replace accepted_mode_a_truth_tiers with invented_super_gold and trusted re-sign.
confirmed_gaps.interpretation: Unknown or stronger-sounding authority tiers are accepted as nonempty strings rather than resolved through the closed producer authority lattice.

confirmed_gaps.probe: Give a required capability a label absent from required_labels and trusted re-sign.
confirmed_gaps.interpretation: No normalization proves per-capability modes/labels/artifacts are consistent with the top-level required envelope or that required and optional semantics are complete.

confirmed_gaps.probe: Keep a required schema name but substitute its schema ID, relative path and SHA-256 with well-formed invented values, then trusted re-sign.
confirmed_gaps.interpretation: The closed name set is enforced, but exact producer schema/path/hash compatibility depends on the missing 09.07 crosswalk/admission decision.

implementation_packet.objective: Implement an additive deterministic producer admission decision for the frozen signed Main requirements, with use-time freshness, replay evidence, exact crosswalk/trust/authority resolution and normalized required-versus-optional capability coverage.
implementation_packet.path_state: All five allowed paths are absent and clean. Candidate paths are read-only; src/maskfactory/validation.py is dirty in the preserved 08.05 scope and is excluded from mutation. Allowed paths are disjoint from all held 09.01-09.07 and 10.01 units.
acceptance_criteria: Emit a strict canonical self-hashed admission decision binding the exact requirements ID/hash, signer/trust record, producer release, adopted crosswalk, authority policy, revocation head and decision time.

Validate authentication at producer use time, require unexpired active-role trust, and consume an explicit durable nonce/body ledger; same nonce with different body fails and exact replay returns an idempotent prior-decision reference rather than re-admission.

Resolve every accepted schema ID/path/version/hash through the adopted crosswalk and producer release catalog; unknown, duplicate, substituted, unsupported-major or unlisted-minor identities fail closed.

Resolve embedded key-set/rotation/revocation identities against producer-adopted exact bytes and reject missing roles, invalid time windows, revoked/suspended keys, self-asserted trust or disagreement.

Normalize access modes, labels, artifact kinds, media scopes, person counts, transforms, authority floors, evidence and resource envelopes; reject unknown values and contradictions between per-capability and top-level declarations.
Use the closed producer authority lattice; a consumer cannot create, rename or strengthen a truth/authority tier or accept a certificate kind above its exact producer meaning.

Return deterministic required_met, required_unmet, optional_met and optional_unmet sets with ordered closed reasons; optional-only gaps cannot become a required blocker and required gaps cannot be hidden as optional.

Negative tests cover expired/not-yet-valid use, nonce collision/replay, wrong/revoked role, trust-set substitution, schema drift, unknown authority, capability-envelope contradiction, ambiguous versions and resource-budget contradictions.

Frozen v1 schemas, fixtures, validation.py and all held worker paths remain byte-identical. Main authors the signed requirements; MaskFactory only validates/adopts or rejects them and never fabricates consumer intent.

observed_verification.command: four trusted re-sign probes: embedded key set, authority tier, capability/top-level labels, accepted schema identity
python -m pytest -q tests/test_mask_bridge_contracts_v1.py -k 'positive_documents or trust_anchor_role_revocation or fixture_adoption_replay'

claim_boundary: This audit proves the frozen document is authenticated and structurally strong but lacks the producer use-time admission/replay/normalization decision. It does not complete MF-P6-10.03, publish Main requirements, adopt a release, or claim runtime/end-to-end compatibility.
